content.text: Answer four problems. Write your solutions clearly in complete English sentences. You may quote results (within reason) as long as you state them clearly.

body.text: Let \(R\) be a commutative ring with \(1\) and let \(I, J, P\) be ideals in \(R\) such that \(I \cap J \subset P\).

subparts.label: (a)
subparts.text: Prove that if \(P\) is a prime ideal then either \(I \subset P\) or \(J \subset P\).

subparts.label: (b)
subparts.text: Give an example which shows that if \(P\) is not prime then the conclusion above may not hold.

body.text: Prove that the ring \(\mathbb{Z}[\sqrt{-2}] = \{a + b\sqrt{-2} : a,b \in \mathbb{Z}\}\) is a Euclidean domain with respect to the norm defined by \(N(a+b\sqrt{-2})=a^2+2b^2\) for \(a,b \in \mathbb{Z}\).
Prove that the following polynomials are irreducible over \(\mathbb{Q}\). Cite any theorems that you use.

subparts.label: (a)
subparts.text: State the First Isomorphism Theorem for \(R\)-modules.

subparts.label: (b)
subparts.text:
Let \(A_1,A_2,\ldots,A_n\) be \(R\)-modules and for \(1 \leq i \leq n\) let \(B_i\) be a submodule of \(A_i\). Prove that
\[
(A_1 \times \cdots \times A_n)/(B_1 \times \cdots \times B_n) \cong (A_1/B_1) \times \cdots \times (A_n/B_n).
\]

body.text: State the classification theorems for finitely generated modules over a PID. You should give both the invariant factor and the elementary divisor decompositions. Be sure to include the uniqueness statements.
Let \(L/K\) be an algebraic field extension and let \(R\) be a subring of \(L\) such that \(K \subset R\). Prove that \(R\) is a field.
